title: Models and prompts live as seat-keyed DATA, separate from script
rules: seat identity (provider, durable strengths/weaknesses, preferred roles, prompt framing) lives in seats.json — a future model takes a seat by editing DATA, never a script
prompt text lives in prompts.json keyed by PURPOSE (approval, review, daedalus, team_build, node_build, decompose), binding by {seat}/{role} placeholders
no council/workshop prompt template may contain a literal seat name — the seat is always a variable, so role-strength placement survives model succession
role->seat bindings (parallel_authorship, workshop_seats) are data in seats.json; the daedalus module exports them from there
model IDS stay env/per-call (ANTHROPIC_MODEL, OPENAI_MODEL, XAI_MODEL, GEMINI_MODEL) — a seat names a role-shaped slot, never a snapshot id
load-bearing contract language inside a prompt (review check-registry rules, daedalus no-objection-hash rule) changes only with its governing decision (CHANGE-PROTOCOL)
why: The council's value is placing each model where it is strongest. If prompts or role bindings hardcode a model name in script, succession requires code review of orchestration logic; as seat-keyed data, a stronger future model slots into constraints/implementation/adversary/verifier seats by a reviewed data edit, and every prompt it inherits already invokes the seat's purpose.
change_rule: Adding a seat, moving a role binding, or changing prompt text is a data change to seats.json/prompts.json reviewed on its own terms; changing HOW templates are loaded/interpolated is a mechanism change. The two must not be mixed in one edit without calling it out.

oracle.test: build-gate npm test (test-teams: leads placed by preferred_roles; test-team-prompts: frames/instructions reach the wire; test-daedalus: seat/role bindings drive the workshop)
oracle.contract_equals_code: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (MODEL_PROFILES/EFFORT_TIERS/PARALLEL_ROLES/DAEDALUS_SEATS exports deep-equal the JSON; profileFor probed against every seat frame; seat set equals the seat-registry council seats; every council-purpose template is seat-name-free and carries the {seat} placeholder)
